title: Treasury Sanctions Nicaraguan Officials Enabling the Murillo-Ortega Dictatorship's Repression
summary: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned five Nicaraguan government officials on February 26, 2026, including leadership of Nicaragua's Financial Analysis Unit (UAF), Ministry of Labor, telecommunications regulator TELCOR, and the Nicaraguan Army's Directorate of Military Intelligence and Counterintelligence (DICIM), citing their roles in enabling political repression and human rights abuses under the Murillo-Ortega dictatorship.
key_points: OFAC designated five Nicaraguan officials pursuant to Executive Order 13851, as amended by E.O. 14088, for being officials of the Government of Nicaragua on or after January 10, 2007

Sanctioned individuals include: Major General Denis Membreno Rivas and Commissioner Aldo Martin Saenz Ulloa (Directors of the Financial Analysis Unit since 2012), Minister of Labor Johana Vanessa Flores Jimenez (appointed August 2025), Deputy Director General Celia Margarita Reyes Ochoa of TELCOR (since 2023), and Major General Leonel Jose Gutierrez Lopez of DICIM

The Treasury alleges Nicaragua's Financial Analysis Unit is chaired by active military and police officers loyal to Ortega, allowing the regime to use AML/CFT frameworks to persecute political opponents
TELCOR's 2024 General Law on Convergent Telecommunications granted authorities extensive powers to collect private data, intercept communications, and geolocate individuals
All property and interests in property of designated individuals in U.S. possession must be blocked and reported to OFAC; entities 50% or more owned by blocked persons are also blocked
Financial institutions risk sanctions exposure for engaging in certain transactions involving designated persons
why_it_matters: The why_it_matters field should not speculate on market impact. Replace with: 'This sanctions action targets key enablers of Nicaragua's authoritarian governance structure—financial intelligence, labor enforcement, and communications surveillance. Assets of designated individuals in U.S. possession are frozen and most transactions with them by U.S. persons are prohibited. No market impact discussion is provided in the source.'
important_numbers: January 10, 2007 - baseline date for E.O. 13851 designation
August 2025 - Flores appointed Minister of Labor
2023 - Reyes became TELCOR deputy director general
2012 - UAF created; Membreno and Saenz have led since inception
